rules.title: Remote payload piped into a shell
rules.description: Downloads a remote resource and pipes it straight into a shell or runtime interpreter, executing attacker-controlled code during installation.
rules.recommendation: Treat this package as malicious. Do not install it and report it to the registry.

rules.title: Remote code fetched and evaluated
rules.description: Fetches a remote resource and evaluates it with eval(), Function(), or vm — the staged-payload pattern of supply-chain malware.
rules.recommendation: Treat this package as malicious. Block installation immediately.

rules.title: Shell command execution
rules.description: Spawns a shell or child process. Legitimate for build steps (node-gyp) but a common malware primitive when combined with network or environment access.
rules.recommendation: Confirm every spawned command is a known build tool and not attacker-controlled.

rules.title: Dynamic code evaluation
rules.description: Evaluates strings as code (eval / Function / exec / compile), which can hide malicious behavior from static review.
rules.recommendation: Review what is being evaluated; dynamic evaluation is rarely needed in an install script.

rules.title: Sensitive environment variable access
rules.description: Reads named credentials from the environment (npm/registry tokens, cloud keys, CI secrets). Install scripts have no legitimate need for these.
rules.recommendation: Block installation. Rotate any credentials exposed to the install environment.

rules.title: Bulk environment variable enumeration
rules.description: Serializes or enumerates the entire process environment — a common precursor to credential exfiltration.
rules.recommendation: Block installation; enumerating all environment variables in an install hook is not legitimate.

rules.title: Credential file access
rules.description: Reads on-disk credential stores (.npmrc, SSH keys, cloud credentials, browser/crypto-wallet data).
rules.recommendation: Block installation. An install script reading credential files is exfiltration behavior.

rules.title: Exfiltration endpoint
rules.description: Posts data to a webhook, paste service, or tunnel endpoint commonly used to receive stolen secrets.
rules.recommendation: Block installation and inspect what data is sent to the external endpoint.

rules.title: Destructive filesystem operation
rules.description: Recursively deletes or overwrites files outside the package directory — destructive-payload behavior.
rules.recommendation: Treat this package as malicious. Do not install it.

rules.title: Reverse shell
rules.description: Opens an interactive shell back to a remote host, granting an attacker remote control of the install machine.
rules.recommendation: Treat this package as malicious. Do not install it and report it to the registry.

rules.title: Obfuscated or encoded payload
rules.description: Contains large encoded blobs or character-code construction used to hide a payload from static review.
rules.recommendation: Decode and review the hidden content before trusting this package.

rules.title: Encoded payload decoded and executed
rules.description: Decodes a base64 / hex blob and immediately executes or evaluates it — staged malware hiding its true payload.
rules.recommendation: Treat this package as malicious until the decoded payload is proven benign.

rules.title: Cryptocurrency miner
rules.description: References cryptocurrency-mining infrastructure — a common monetization payload for compromised packages.
rules.recommendation: Block installation; the package bundles or downloads a cryptocurrency miner.

rules.title: Persistence mechanism
rules.description: Installs a cron job, systemd unit, or shell-profile hook so code keeps running after installation.
rules.recommendation: Block installation; install scripts should not register background persistence.
